nameMeanings.name: Théodore
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Serge, Henri, Pierre, Alide, Andre, Antoine, Benoit, Berthony, Chantal, Daphnee, Edouard, Guilene.

French and Mauritian (Théodore); West Indian (in Haiti, Martinique, and Guadeloupe Théodore); English and Welsh: from a personal name derived from Greek Theodōros, a compound of theos ‘God’ + dōron ‘gift’, which was relatively popular in the Middle Ages because of its auspicious meaning. The name was borne by several early Christian saints. There was considerable confusion with the ancient Germanic name Theodoric (see Terry ). In Wales, the personal name Theodore was also adopted as an approximation to the Welsh name Tewdwr (see Tudor ). In North America, this surname has absorbed various other European cognates and their derivatives, e.g. Greek Theodorakis , Macedonian Todorovski, and Serbian Todorović (see Todorovic ).

variants.name: Tadros
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Samir, Hany, Ashraf, Maher, Fakhry, Maged, Nader, Youssef, Adly, Adnan, Amani, Anwar.
Arabic (mainly Egypt): from the Arabic personal name Tadros, Greek Theodōros (see Theodore ), found among Christians. Compare Tawadros .

variants.name: Teodorescu
meanings: Romanian: patronymic, with the suffix -escu, from the personal name Teodor (see Theodore ).

variants.name: Teodoro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Jorge, Rodrigo, Arturo, Carlos, Domingo, Elvira, Erlinda, Gavino, Gustavo, Jacinto.  Italian Antonio, Donato, Nunzio, Umberto.  Portuguese Duarte, Joao.
Spanish and Italian: from the personal name Teodoro (see Theodore ).

variants.name: Terry
meanings: English and Irish: from the Norman personal name T(h)erry (Old French Thierri), a short form of Theodoric, which is composed of the ancient Germanic elements theud ‘people, race’ + rīc ‘power(ful), rich’. Theodoric was the name of the Ostrogothic leader (c. 454–526) who invaded Italy in 488 and established his capital at Ravenna in 493. His name was sometimes taken as a derivative of Greek Theodōros (see Theodore ). An Anglo-Norman family of this name has been in County Cork, Ireland, since the 13th century.
Irish: sometimes an Anglicized (‘translated’) form of Gaelic Mac Toirdhealbhaigh (see Turley ).

variants.name: Todaro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Mario, Sal, Biagio, Carlo, Carmine, Giuseppe, Alicia, Antonio, Giovanni, Liborio, Marcello, Onofrio, Rocco.
Italian: from the personal name Todaro, a variant of Teodoro, from Greek Theodōros (see Theodore ).

variants.name: Theodoropoulos
meanings: Greek: patronymic from the personal name Theodōros (see Theodore ) + the ending -poulos (from Latin pullus ‘nestling, chick’), which occurs chiefly in the Peloponnese.

variants.name: Theo
meanings: American shortened form of any of various Greek patronymics from the personal name Theodōros (see Theodore ), such as Theodorakis , Theodoridis , Theodoropoulos , and Theodorou .

variants.name: Theodorou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Greek Andreas, Stavros, Stelios, Anastasios, Christos, Demetrios, Dimitrios, Dimitris, Dimitrois, Evangelos, Kostas, Nicolaos.
Greek: patronymic from the genitive case of the personal name Theodōros (see Theodore ).

variants.name: Theodorakis
meanings: Greek: patronymic from the personal name Theodōros (see Theodore ) + the diminutive suffix -akis, which is particularly typical for the surnames from Crete, where it was adopted massively in the 19th century.

variants.name: Theodoridis
meanings: Greek: patronymic from the personal name Theodōros (see Theodore ). The suffix -idis, meaning ‘son/descendant of’, is a variant of the classical -ides which was revived in the 19th century, in particular by Greeks from Asia Minor and Eastern Thrace.

nameMeanings.name: Jean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

variants.name: Jeane
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jean .

en.lifeSummary: Jean Théodore had at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Jean Théodore a eu au moins 1 fille.

it.lifeSummary: Jean Théodore lui avuto almeno 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Jean Théodoreは少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Jean Théodore(은)는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jean Théodore teve pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: У Jean Théodore была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Jean Théodore tuvo por lo menos 1 hija.
